title: EDUCATION - 211info
text: VENEZUELA'S VOICE IN OREGON EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides virtual education focusing on the Hispanic/Latino community. Educational workshops include Spanish language classes, financial literacy workshops, assisting job seekers with creating resumes, and mental health workshops. Also helps families navigate the educational system and advocate for equitable access to school resources and services through culturally affirming workshops and outreach. Eligibility: -Mental health workshops: Age 18 and older who identify as immigrants or refugees -All other services: Age 18 and older who identify as Hispanic/Latino Hours: Varies by event Email Visit Website Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Arts and Crafts Instruction * Hispanic/Latino Community Financial Literacy Training * Hispanic/Latino Community Prejob Guidance * Hispanic/Latino Community Spanish as a Second Language * Hispanic/Latino Community Workshops/Symposiums * Hispanic/Latino Community Workshops/Symposiums * Immigrants * Mental Health Issues Workshops/Symposiums * Mental Health Issues * Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Other Services Offered At This Location: ARTS AND CULTURE - CULTURE INFORMATION SERVICES - HEALTH CONNECTIONS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
